title: Boston Made the World's Biggest Soccer Ball — 47 Feet Wide
script: Biggest soccer ball ever? Boston's Massport just broke the Guinness World Record for the world's largest soccer ball — 47.9 feet across. That's roughly the height of a 5-story building. The old record was 39.98 feet, set in Qatar back in 2013. Massport blew past it. They inflated this thing at Piers Park II in East Boston — you walk up to it, you feel small. It's printed with silhouettes of every continent on Earth, a tribute to the countries descending on Boston for the World Cup. Boston is hosting 7 matches at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough this summer, and this ball is basically a billboard that cannot be missed. It's only there until June 18. Can you even kick a 47.9-foot soccer ball?
